mnemonics.title: DECIDE Framework for Decision Making
mnemonics.content: DECIDE: D-evaluate condition, E-xecute true block, C-heck else-if chains, I-nest when needed, D-ecide between if-else and ternary, E-nd properly with braces

mnemonics.title: Truth Value Reminder
mnemonics.content: ANY NON-ZERO MEANS TRUE - REMEMBER: POSITIVE, NEGATIVE, BOTH COUNT AS TRUE; ONLY ZERO IS FALSE

mnemonics.title: Assignment vs Comparison
mnemonics.content: ONE EQUAL MEANS ASSIGN (GIVE), TWO EQUALS MEANS COMPARE (CHECK) - EQUAL COMPARE PREVENTS BUGS

acronyms.expansion: If Inside If - Conditional Check Within Conditional Check
acronyms.usage: Used when multiple dependent conditions need to be evaluated sequentially

acronyms.expansion: Multiple Else-If Blocks - Sequential Condition Testing
acronyms.usage: Used when checking mutually exclusive conditions from most specific to general
Used as a compact alternative to simple if-else statements

visualTips.visualization: Draw a diamond shape for decision (condition) with two paths: YES/TRUE path leading to one box, NO/FALSE path leading to another box. This shows branching clearly.
Think of a ladder or staircase: start at bottom, check first step, if not true move up to next step, continue until finding a true step or falling through to final else.
Think of a traffic signal: Question mark (?) is the signal asking a question, if TRUE take the left path (before colon), if FALSE take the right path (after colon).

analogies.analogy: Like a checkpoint or security gate: if you have the right ID (condition true), you pass through; otherwise you don't enter that area.
Like a fork in the road: one path when condition is true, another path when condition is false - you definitely take exactly one path.
Like a series of checkpoints at an airport: first check visa, if fails check passport, if fails check ID, if all fail then deny entry - multiple selective gates.
Like a vending machine: you insert money (condition), if enough (true) you get product A, otherwise you get your money back (false result).
